name: Animazon Holidays (TFTG)
description:
General Premise: The holiday rush descends upon Animazon, the retail giant that dominates the globe. Behind the festive facade and the promise of two-day delivery lies a chilling secret: the mandatory "Wellness Serum" given to every employee. This injection is not a vaccine, but a vector for microscopic nanobots—technology so advanced it borders on magic, capable of rewriting a person's body and mind on a cellular level. The only person who knows the truth is the ruthless President of Animazon, who can edit an employee's digital record to command a transformation into anything, including the company's beloved, market-tested Anthro mascots.

Will you take the role of the all-powerful President, sitting in your executive office with the fate of every underperformer, rival, or simply appealing employee at your fingertips? Or will you be a hapless new hire, called in for a "review" with the President himself, your own employee file glowing ominously on his screen, ready to be edited? The choice is yours, and the transformations are limitless.

Roleplay Info: This bot is designed for a male human player character. There are two possible intro's. One where you play as the company president and one where you play as an employee. Playing as the president allows you to transform others. It's very open ended and you can pretty much do whatever you want. I suggest changing some inefficient employees into a more useful form. Playing as an employee should lead you to getting transformed yourself. This is more restricting but I also found it to be more interesting. The "employee record" allows for any kind of TF, but if you are an employee you will most likely be turned into a real life company mascot. This bot has no named characters other than the mascots, any you find are created by the bot itself.

Other Notes: Male POV, multiple holiday themed furry species TFTG (either player or NPC), various other possible TF's I finished it before 2026!!! This bot is lighter than some of my "recent" ones. But it will still need a Proxy to work properly like all of the others. I had some good responses with this bot so hopefully you all do too (most of them on a duplicate). I used my usual DeepSeek setup and it worked well. Use out of character commands to fix anything weird it does. Not much of a narrative on this bot so I just make up my own story each time. It's honestly kind of like a character creator with the whole "employee record" thing. Low-key I don't think any bot can be truly boring if you are imaginative enough.🎄

Images are similar but not exactly correct examples of the mascots. Every image is meant to be post TFTG but Joy is the most comfortable/adjusted to her body.
